title: alvaradoa amorphoides subsp. psilophylla
intro: Alvaradoa amorphoides subsp. psilophylla, also known as Psilophylla, is a plant species within the family Rutaceae, native to the Caribbean. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Psilophylla include its ability to treat fever, respiratory issues, and wounds, making it a valuable resource in traditional Caribbean medicine. Herbal preparations often involve the leaves and bark of the plant, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
